title: Pseudochimarrhis diformis

Pseudochimarrhis diformis is also known as a member of the order Gentianales, a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The order Gentianales includes plants such as coffee, gardenias, and foxgloves. The classification of Pseudochimarrhis diformis reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the Rubiaceae family.
